model_narrative: We move a product from several plants to several terminals. Each plant-to-terminal route has a known shipping cost per unit, and some routes also need a number of tankers per unit shipped. We decide how many units to send on each route. We also track the total shipping cost, the total number of tankers used, and the overall cost of the plan. The objective is to make the overall cost as small as possible.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the plants that ship the product out

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the terminals that receive the product

params.name: c
params.doc: shipping cost per unit on a plant-to-terminal route, in dollars per unit

params.name: t
params.doc: number of tankers needed per unit shipped on a plant-to-terminal route; most routes need none

params.name: a
params.doc: amount of product available at each plant, in units

params.name: b
params.doc: amount of product required at each terminal, in units

params.name: ctank
params.doc: cost charged per tanker used, in dollars per tanker

params.name: cship
params.doc: weight applied to the shipping cost component when forming total cost, a unitless multiplier

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the number of units shipped from a plant to a terminal

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: total cost of the plan, in dollars

vars.name: tank
vars.doc: total number of tankers used across all routes

vars.name: ship
vars.doc: total shipping cost across all routes, in dollars

description: The overall cost of the plan combines the total shipping cost and the total tanker usage. Take the total shipping cost weighted by its multiplier and add the total tankers used priced at the per-tanker charge, and set the overall cost equal to that sum.
expected_pyomo: model.defcost = Constraint(expr=model.cost == model.cship * model.ship + model.ctank * model.tank)

model_narrative: We move a product from several plants to several terminals. Each plant-to-terminal route has a known shipping cost per unit, and some routes also need a number of tankers per unit shipped. We decide how many units to send on each route. We also track the total shipping cost, the total number of tankers used, and the overall cost of the plan. The objective is to make the overall cost as small as possible.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the plants that ship the product out

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the terminals that receive the product

params.name: c
params.doc: shipping cost per unit on a plant-to-terminal route, in dollars per unit

params.name: t
params.doc: number of tankers needed per unit shipped on a plant-to-terminal route; most routes need none

params.name: a
params.doc: amount of product available at each plant, in units

params.name: b
params.doc: amount of product required at each terminal, in units

params.name: ctank
params.doc: cost charged per tanker used, in dollars per tanker

params.name: cship
params.doc: weight applied to the shipping cost component when forming total cost, a unitless multiplier

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the number of units shipped from a plant to a terminal

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: total cost of the plan, in dollars

vars.name: tank
vars.doc: total number of tankers used across all routes

vars.name: ship
vars.doc: total shipping cost across all routes, in dollars

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, the overall cost of the plan combines the total shipping cost and the total tanker usage. Take the total shipping cost weighted by its multiplier and add the total tankers used priced at the per-tanker charge, and set the overall cost equal to that sum. Second, the total shipping cost adds up the per-unit shipping cost on each route multiplied by the units sent on that route, summed over every plant and terminal. Set the total shipping cost equal to that amount. Third, the total number of tankers used adds up the tankers needed per unit on each route multiplied by the units sent on that route, summed over every plant and terminal. Set the total tankers used equal to that amount. Fourth, no plant can send out more than what it has available. For each plant, the total units shipped from it to all terminals must not exceed the amount available at that plant. Finally, every terminal's requirement must be met. For each terminal, the total units shipped into it from all plants must be at least the amount required at that terminal.
expected_pyomo:
model.defcost = Constraint(expr=model.cost == model.cship * model.ship + model.ctank * model.tank)
model.defship = Constraint(expr=model.ship == sum(model.c[i, j] * model.x[i, j] for i in model.i for j in model.j))
model.deftank = Constraint(expr=model.tank == sum(model.t[i, j] * model.x[i, j] for i in model.i for j in model.j))
def supply_rule(model, i):
    return sum(model.x[i, j] for j in model.j) <= model.a[i]
model.supply = Constraint(model.i, rule=supply_rule)
def demand_rule(model, j):
    return sum(model.x[i, j] for i in model.i) >= model.b[j]
model.demand = Constraint(model.j, rule=demand_rule)
